missing_blockers: For genetics cohorts, record family history consistently, ancestry, sex assigned at birth, age at onset, and whether relatives are part of the recruitment plan.

cohort_tier: Tier C: exploratory cohort; standardization needed before primary analysis
missing_blockers: Complete all four core pattern fields: bilateral symmetry, disproportion, hand/foot sparing, and pain/tenderness.; Record differential diagnosis review before using as a confirmed-case research record.; Complete BMI, WHtR, and WHR inputs for standardized anthropometry.; Add a numeric pain/tenderness score for symptom-severity comparisons.; Add limb circumference or comparable limb-measurement fields.; Record onset/worsening window, especially for hormone-window or genetics studies.; Self-reported-only cases should be separated from clinician-adjudicated confirmed-case analyses.; Select explicit comparator groups before recruitment so case-control differences are interpretable.
noise_sources: Self-report reliance; Differential review missing; Obesity/body-composition context; Staging/type variability; Pain measurement variability; Absent limb measurements; Comparator ambiguity

cohort_tier: Tier B: usable cohort with required strata
missing_blockers: Complete all four core pattern fields: bilateral symmetry, disproportion, hand/foot sparing, and pain/tenderness.; Record differential diagnosis review before using as a confirmed-case research record.; Add lymphatic/fluid assessment fields such as ICG, BIS, ultrasound, lymphoscintigraphy, or MRI if available.
